name: Perplexity Chart System
description: Four-surface chart system for social treatments. Same 6 archetypes render on 4 surfaces. Tokens flip; spacing scale, type ramp, and frame structure stay locked.

academic-dark.label: Surface I · academic (dark)
academic-dark.intent: Pure black canvas. Warm-white hero. Slate baseline. Maximum value contrast. Paper figures, research blog, internal benchmarks.

editorial-dark.label: Surface II · editorial (dark)
editorial-dark.intent: Warm near-black canvas. Warm-cream hero. Warm-grey baseline. Restrained warmth. NYT/FT print, magazine spread, brand moments, social posts.

academic-light.label: Surface III · academic (light)
academic-light.intent: Warm paper canvas (#FBFAF4). Near-black ink. Slate baseline preserved. Print-ready, research paper figures, light-mode product.

editorial-light.label: Surface IV · editorial (light)
editorial-light.intent: Warm cream paper (#f4ead4). Warm near-black ink. Mid warm-grey baseline. Print editorial, newspaper figure, magazine spread on light.

type-rules.hero-color: Hero stat sits in --text (var --text-primary). Never in accent.
type-rules.one-accent-per-card: Maximum one accent per card. Don't double-accent.
type-rules.hero-figures: Display heroes use proportional figures: font-feature-settings: 'pnum' 1, 'lnum' 1, 'kern' 1
type-rules.stacked-figures: Stacked columns use tabular figures: font-feature-settings: 'tnum' 1, 'lnum' 1, 'kern' 1
type-rules.unit-chrome: Unit glyphs (%, pp, pt, x) must never punch below hero baseline. font-size: 0.46em; vertical-align: 0.34em; line-height: 1; display: inline-block;
type-rules.no-em-dashes: Use periods, commas, or separate sentences. No em dashes in copy.
type-rules.no-serif-in-utility: Sans + Mono only for utility/tool UIs. Serif reserved for editorial heroes.

A.name: Stat + bar chart
A.use: Single hero stat with comparison bars and Pareto bracket

B.name: Pin spike
B.use: 50-pin distribution with one hero peak and inline callout chip

content-contract.density-underflow: swap to A-card or amplify peak isolation

C.name: Compact 2x2 comparison
C.use: Four metrics in a 2x2 grid, each with stat + sparkline

D.name: Dot-matrix bar
D.use: Wide dot-matrix distribution with peak column

E.name: Editorial stat block
E.use: Display stat with deck, sparkline, and 3-col metric quad

F.name: Story stack wallet list
F.use: Vertical story-frame with goal progress and benchmark list

G.name: Editorial header card
G.use: Title-only, essay-opener, or quote card. No stat, no chart, no list. Use when the headline IS the content. Three sub-variants by headline character.

typography.title-face: GT Canon Narrow Medium (NOT PPLX Mono). Title IS the content; it gets editorial display type, the same role GT Canon plays as hero stat on data cards. Mono is reserved for utility chrome (kicker, footer) only.

G-display.title-face-detail: GT Canon Narrow Medium (wdth 75, wght 500, opsz 60–144)

G-editorial.use: Essay opener or longer headline. Reads as editorial type, not display.

G-quote.title-face-detail: GT Canon Italic (wdth 100, wght 500, italic, opsz 48–96)

G.title-scale-source: fit.js measures title text length, computes a scale factor so the title fills 60–85% of canvas height. Bound to t-title's font-size via --fit-title-scale. Hero-scale, row-pad, and section-gap are unused on G and ignored if set.
G.note: Introduced in v7 · 2026-05-22 to absorb the degenerate header-only failure mode found in the edge-case stress test. The original test used PPLX Mono for the title, which was wrong: Mono is utility only. Editorial cards use the same display face (GT Canon) that data cards use for their hero stat — the title is the hero on G.

frame-rules.even-margins: Left/right margins must be equal. Non-negotiable.
frame-rules.viewport-spanning-gradients: If any gradient effect is used, it spans the viewport. Not per-element.

failure-detection.purpose: The 5-regime CCR balancer is a one-dimensional diagnostic. Three classes of content can break it silently. fit.js detects these and emits warnings + visible data attributes. Authors fix manually — the system never auto-mitigates.

horizontal-overflow.detection: For chart labels, list-row names, and titles: measure rendered text width. If text-width > containing-cell-width * 0.95, flag.
horizontal-overflow.console: warn: [pplx-fit] horizontal overflow in <selector> — label length exceeds column width
horizontal-overflow.author-fix: Truncate, abbreviate, rotate, or pick a different archetype.

density-mismatch.signal: data-fit-warn="density-mismatch" on the offending content block
density-mismatch.detection: Read data-fit-density on chart containers. If declared density < archetype.content-contract.min-density, flag.
density-mismatch.console: warn: [pplx-fit] visual density 0.18 below B-card minimum 0.25 — chart will read as empty
density-mismatch.author-fix: Add more data, raise the minimum baseline, or swap to a different archetype.

missing-structure.detection: For each block in archetype.required-blocks, check the card has a matching [data-fit-content]. If not, flag.
missing-structure.console: warn: [pplx-fit] archetype F requires block 'list' but no [data-fit-content="list"] found
missing-structure.author-fix: Add the required structure, or pick a different archetype (e.g. G for header-only cards).

failure-detection.visible-warnings: When data-fit-warn is set on a card and the page declares a .fit-debug class on the body, fit.js renders a small magenta strip on the card with the warning text. Default state: warnings only in console.

layout-balancer.problem: Cards have fixed canvas (1080×1920, 1080×1080, etc) but variable content. Static CSS cannot choose the right spacing strategy because it depends on how much content there actually is.
layout-balancer.solution: fit.js measures content-to-canvas ratio (CCR) at runtime, classifies it, and applies a strategy via data-fit attribute + CSS variables. CSS owns the look; JS owns the strategy.

amplify.intent: Content too sparse. Grow the hero, expand hierarchy, widen section gaps.

distribute.intent: Room available. Distribute remaining space proportionally with subtle hero growth.

anchor.intent: Content fits comfortably. No adjustment, the locked design rhythm reads correctly.

pack.intent: Content fills canvas. No adjustment, content is exactly where it should be.

compact.intent: Content overflows. Tighten type, reduce row padding, shrink hero, dim auxiliary chrome.

contract.data-fit-card: Set on the card root <section>. fit.js measures and tags this element.
contract.data-fit-content: Mark each natural-height content block inside the card. Used as measurement targets.
contract.data-fit-ccr: Written by fit.js: the actual measured ratio, e.g. '0.86'.
contract.--fit-hero-scale: CSS variable to multiply hero font-size. Default 1.
contract.--fit-row-pad: CSS variable to multiply list-row padding. Default 1.
contract.--fit-section-gap: CSS variable to multiply section row-gap. Default 1.
contract.--fit-aux-opacity: CSS variable for auxiliary chrome opacity in compact mode. Default 1.

non-negotiables: The balancer never breaks the design contract.
PPLX mark stays 36×36 top-right at all strategies.
Card padding stays --s-7 (56px) at all strategies.
The balancer only flexes WITHIN those constraints. Hero scale, row padding, section gap are the only knobs.
